Products

GFS — Guided Fault Search

Provides guided diagnostic decision trees for Porsche workshop technicians. Consists of ~30 microservices deployed as AWS Lambda functions behind API Gateway, with a CloudFront-hosted frontend authenticated via PPN (Porsche Partner Net).

PDA — Porsche Diagnostic Agent

AI-powered automotive diagnostic system using AWS Bedrock AgentCore and LangGraph for multi-step reasoning. Provides an Angular frontend and Python-based AI agents for intelligent fault diagnosis.

Digital Solution Intent (Auto-Generated):
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

The GFS Digital Solution provides diagnostic tooling for Porsche aftersales workshops. It consists of two IT products:

  • GFS (Guided Fault Search): A serverless platform delivering structured diagnostic decision trees to workshop technicians via a web-based interface. Technicians are guided through fault isolation procedures step by step.
  • PDA (Porsche Diagnostic Agent): An AI-powered diagnostic assistant that uses large language models (AWS Bedrock) and multi-step reasoning (LangGraph) to provide intelligent, context-aware diagnostic recommendations.

Both products share infrastructure patterns (AWS CDK, GitLab CI commons) and authentication (PPN via Cognito federation).
Compliance (Auto-Generated):
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

Security Scanning

All repositories include a vulnerability-scan stage in their CI pipeline as the first gate before any build or deployment occurs.

Dependency Auditing

npm-based repositories enforce dependency auditing:

  • Default audit level: moderate
  • PDA UI uses critical level (due to bundled transitive dependencies in aws-cdk-lib)
  • Audit reports generated as HTML artifacts

FOSS Components

Key open-source dependencies identified across the solution:

CategoryKey Dependencies
Runtime (Node.js)Express, AWS SDK v3, AWS CDK
FrontendAngular, Porsche Design System
AI/ML (Python)LangGraph, Anthropic SDK, Boto3
TestingJest, Cypress, pytest

Authentication & Authorization

  • User authentication via AWS Cognito federated with PPN (Porsche Partner Net)
  • Service authentication via IAM Roles (one per service, assumed in CI pipeline)
  • Frontend cookie-based session via Lambda@Edge authenticator

Detailed security concept (SKO) documentation and FOSS license compliance reports should be maintained separately as per PASS Minimum Standard ID 10 and ID 12.
Data (Auto-Generated):
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

Data Storage Systems

SystemServiceData Type
DynamoDBgff-cloud-adminConfiguration data, admin settings
DynamoDB/RDSgff-cloud-databaseCore application data
S3VariousStatic assets, publications, diagnostic files
RDSgff-cloud-prisma-masterdataVehicle master data (from PVH Kafka)

Data Sources

  • Vehicle master data: Consumed from PVH via Kafka events (pcds-masterdata-service)
  • Diagnostic decision trees: Imported via XSLT parser (gff-cloud-xslt-parser)
  • Workshop publications: Managed via CRS (Content Retrieval Service)

Database Migrations

Schema migrations are handled by the dedicated GFS/gff-cloud-db-migration repository using AWS CDK.

Detailed data classification, retention policies, and deletion concepts should be documented per PASS Minimum Standards for Data Protection and GoBD compliance.

Software Development Culture (Auto-Gener:
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

Branching Strategy

Repositories use a trunk-based development approach with main as the default branch. Merge request pipelines are excluded to avoid duplicate CI runs:

yaml

Code Quality Practices

PracticeToolEnforcement
Static Code AnalysisSonarQubeCI pipeline stage (blocking)
Linting (TypeScript)ESLintCI + local
Code FormattingPrettierCI + pre-commit
Dependency Auditingnpm auditCI pipeline (moderate/critical)
Security ScanningGitLab vulnerability scannerFirst CI stage

Shared Infrastructure

The team maintains shared CI/CD templates in GFS/gff-commons, providing standardized pipeline configurations for AWS deployments. This ensures consistency across all ~40 repositories.

Shared Libraries

  • @gff/cdk-library — Reusable CDK constructs, published to GitLab npm registry
  • @gff/sdk-library — Reusable AWS SDK wrappers
  • @gff/component-library — Shared UI components
Logging and Monitoring (Auto-Generated):
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

Monitoring Infrastructure

As an AWS-based serverless solution, GFS leverages native AWS monitoring services:

  • AWS CloudWatch: Lambda function logs, API Gateway metrics, custom metrics
  • AWS X-Ray: Distributed tracing (where enabled)

Alerting

Alerting is configured via the GFS/gff-cloud-alerts repository (CloudWatch Alarms, SNS notifications).

No dedicated monitoring configuration files (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog) were found in the repositories. Monitoring appears to rely on AWS-native services. Consider documenting:

  • CloudWatch dashboard locations
  • Alert escalation procedures
  • On-call rotation (if applicable)
  • SLA monitoring dashboards
Change Management (Auto-Generated):
Auto-generated from GitLab repositories. Last updated: 2026-04-29. Do not edit sections marked as auto-generated.

Change Process

Changes are managed through GitLab merge requests with CI pipeline validation. The standard flow:

  1. Feature branch created from main
  2. Merge request opened with CI pipeline validation
  3. Code review and approval
  4. Merge to main triggers deployment pipeline (dev → stage → prod)

Automated Quality Gates

Every change must pass before deployment:

  • Vulnerability scan
  • Build success
  • Unit tests pass
  • Static code analysis (SonarQube quality gate)

Version Management

Services use semantic versioning with automated tagging in the CI pipeline (tagging stage). Shared libraries (@gff/cdk-library v1.155.0, @gff/sdk-library v1.230.0) are versioned independently and consumed via GitLab npm registry.

Infrastructure Configuration

All infrastructure is defined as code using AWS CDK (TypeScript). Each service repository contains its own CDK stacks, ensuring configuration is version-controlled alongside application code.

Environment-Specific Configuration

MechanismPurposeExample
CI/CD VariablesIAM Role names per environmentAWS_CLOUD_FRONTEND_IAM_ROLE
AWS SSM Parameter StoreRuntime secrets and configurationPPN client IDs, redirect URLs, cookie domains
DynamoDBApplication configurationAdmin default configs (gff-cloud-admin)
CDK ContextStack-level parametersStack names (API_STACK_NAME)

Shared Configuration

  • CI Templates: GFS/gff-commons provides shared pipeline configuration included by all repositories
  • CDK Constructs: @gff/cdk-library enforces consistent infrastructure patterns
  • Node Options: NODE_OPTIONS: '--max-old-space-size=8192' standardized across CDK repositories
